applicant.name: Michael Hoydich
applicant.signature: signed michael hoydich openai chatgpt 5.5 media 1.5 fast

project.name: PointCast.xyz
project.summary: AI-native broadcast channels, onchain media objects, creator rooms, and token-backed task rewards for visible public participation.

common_packet.one_liner: PointCast turns AI-assisted publishing, rooms, collectibles, and protocol experiments into public tasks people can complete, verify, and earn token-backed credit for.
common_packet.core_paragraph: PointCast.xyz builds AI-native broadcast channels for creators, communities, and protocol ecosystems. It combines agent-assisted production, lightweight web publishing, social distribution, token-backed tasks, and optional onchain artifacts so a single builder can ship useful cultural software at unusual speed.
evidence: Live PointCast web app with public block archive and rooms.
Rewards path defining collect, room, Gandalf, drum, testing, remix, and grant tasks.
Open GitHub repository with shipped routes, workers, and public deployment history.
Onchain and collection surfaces including collect, marketplace, drum, and trophy experiments.
Machine-readable feeds and manifests for blocks, agents, rewards, and grant applications.
common_packet.reward_loop: Grant funding can be turned into visible task pools: participants collect, test, enter rooms, write reports, remix blocks, or improve applications; PointCast records proof and later settles valid work as POINT, DRUM, Tezos, or ecosystem-specific credits.

submission_policy.note: These records are machine-readable drafts for grant preparation. They are not submitted applications until Michael Hoydich reviews and sends them through each program's official channel.

ask.request: API credits to build open-source PointCast task/reward tooling, grant packet generators, and Codex-assisted publishing templates.

applications.fit: The work demonstrates Codex as a collaborator in public software production: static pages, manifests, JSON feeds, reward ledgers, and repeatable grant application scaffolds.

milestones.name: Open-source task schema
milestones.output: Publish JSON schema and examples for PointCast rewards and grant packets.

milestones.name: Codex-assisted workflow guide
milestones.output: Document how Codex turns a brief into live routes, JSON artifacts, and deployment checks.

milestones.name: Reusable starter kit
milestones.output: Release templates for AI-native media pages, task boards, and application manifests.

budget.line_item: OpenAI API usage for generation, review, and structured extraction
Public examples, tests, and deployment verification

deliverables: Codex workflow documentation and public demo routes.
metrics: At least 10 reusable templates or JSON examples.
At least 3 public write-ups documenting Codex-assisted builds.
At least 25 completed reward tasks or test receipts.

ask.request: Support for an AI-native broadcast lab where agents help produce, structure, publish, and measure public media experiments.

applications.fit: PointCast is a visible testbed for human plus AI collaboration: model-assisted content, structured manifests, interactive rooms, and public reward loops.

milestones.name: Broadcast packet v1
milestones.output: Publish repeatable packet format for AI-assisted blocks and rooms.

milestones.name: Public reward pilot
milestones.output: Run a reward pool for useful feedback, testing, and documentation.

milestones.name: Measurement report
milestones.output: Publish results on participation, model usage, and repeat workflows.

metrics: 100 task completions across rooms, collect, drum, and feedback.

ask.request: Claude credits for research, writing, safety review, code review, and structured grant packet generation.

applications.fit: PointCast can show Claude as a careful collaborator for media operations: drafting, triage, structured extraction, grant preparation, and human-readable plus machine-readable publishing.

milestones.name: Claude media operations workflow
milestones.output: Document Claude usage for briefs, grants, rooms, and reward review.

milestones.name: Safety and quality pass
milestones.output: Create checklists for claims, grant language, and public reward descriptions.

milestones.name: Startup case study
milestones.output: Publish a case study on AI-assisted media infrastructure.

deliverables: Published PointCast media ops case study.
metrics: 50 reviewed reward claims or task reports.
10 grant packet drafts structured into JSON.

ask.request: Fund Tezos-native creator broadcasts, collectable PointCast media objects, and a token-backed task pool for collectors and testers.

applications.fit: Tezos has a strong art and experimental publishing culture. PointCast can make Tezos interactions legible through rooms, tasks, collectible drops, and public reward receipts.

milestones.name: Tezos collection pilot
milestones.output: Publish a Tezos-focused collect/drop route with task rewards.

milestones.name: Collector reward pool
milestones.output: Run collect, feedback, and remix rewards with public receipts.

milestones.name: Grant report and templates
milestones.output: Publish measurable results and reusable Tezos launch templates.

deliverables: Public task rewards for collectors and testers.
Open documentation for repeatable Tezos broadcast drops.

ask.request: Support an open reward ledger for public media work, grant transparency, and room-based contribution receipts.

applications.fit: The Ethereum angle is public goods: open schemas, visible reward accounting, contribution receipts, and transparent grant reporting.

milestones.name: Open reward ledger schema
milestones.output: Publish JSON schema for tasks, receipts, reviews, and settlement state.

milestones.name: Public-good pilot
milestones.output: Run a pilot with rooms, docs, testing, and grant-support tasks.

milestones.name: Retro report
milestones.output: Publish results, failures, and reusable tooling.

metrics: 1 exported dataset suitable for other public-good projects.

ask.request: Fund fast consumer-crypto reward experiments for rooms, drum sessions, collecting, and mobile-friendly proof of participation.

applications.fit: Solana is a fit for fast, low-friction consumer interaction. PointCast can test whether tiny rewards pull people into rooms and repeat actions.

milestones.name: Room reward prototype
milestones.output: Publish room tasks with claim receipts and mobile screenshots.

milestones.name: Drum and collect pilot
milestones.output: Reward Drum v7/v8 testing and collect actions.

milestones.name: Consumer crypto report
milestones.output: Publish metrics and UX lessons.

ask.request: Support coinable PointCast broadcast identities, collectible media drops, and reward tasks around creator participation.

applications.fit: Zora is a creator network fit more than a traditional grant path. PointCast can make broadcasts collectible and reward people for participating in the media object.

milestones.name: Coinable broadcast sample
milestones.output: Publish one Zora-native sample channel concept.

milestones.name: Creator reward pilot
milestones.output: Run tasks for collecting, remixing, and sharing a creator broadcast.

milestones.name: Creator economy recap
milestones.output: Publish results and recommendations.

ask.request: Not a grant. Use this machine-readable packet as a company application seed for PointCast as AI-native media infrastructure.

applications.fit: YC should see the company version: AI-native publishing plus task rewards as a new way to make small online communities productive and measurable.

milestones.name: Company thesis
milestones.output: Define the customer, market, wedge, and why now.

milestones.name: Traction packet
milestones.output: Summarize live routes, users, task completions, and reward receipts.

milestones.name: Application draft
milestones.output: Prepare founder answers and demo links.

budget.line_item: No grant budget requested in this record
